Name: Tropius
Effects: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck, and you may reveal any number of Pokémon you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Delicious fruits grew out from around its neck because it always ate the same kind of fruit.

Name: Grubbin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

Name: Fomantis
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Many Trainers give their Fomantis their own flowerpots so they can sunbathe in peace and quiet.

Name: Lurantis ex
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 200 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Poltchageist
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: Place 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

Name: Sinistcha
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: If you have 6 or more Pokémon that have the Hide ‘n’ Sneak Ability in your discard pile, place 4 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home’s floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

Name: Heatran
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Burned Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Mega Delphox ex
Effects: Look at the top 9 cards of your deck, and you may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Sizzlipede
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.

Reveal the bottom 7 cards of your deck, and this attack does 50 damage for each Pokémon you find there that has the Bug Out attack. Then, shuffle any revealed Pokémon back into your deck. Discard the other cards.
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

Name: Armarouge
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any [R] Energy attached.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.

Name: Goldeen
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

Name: Seaking
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

Name: Wailmer
Flavor-Text: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

Name: Wailord ex
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has “Antique” in its name.
Flavor-Text: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

Name: Popplio
Flavor-Text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

Name: Brionne
Flavor-Text: It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try to help.

Name: Primarina
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Also known as a songstress, it is a sight to behold on moonlit nights when it sings in front of the colony it leads.

Name: Finizen
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

Name: Palafin
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This hero of the ocean swims at a speed of 50 knots and saves drowning people and Pokémon.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. This Pokémon’s whole body gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. The Pokémon creates a thundercloud overhead from which to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Charjabug
Flavor-Text: As it digests fallen leaves, it generates and stores electricity, which it can discharge from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon has no Voltaic [L] Energy attached, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

Name: Mega Zeraora ex
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, move up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: You may discard any number of cards from your hand.
Flavor-Text: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 160 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An attached Shellder won’t let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of Slowbro’s tail.

Name: Mega Slowbro ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Jynx
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all attached cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Shuppet
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Flavor-Text: It uses its horn to feed on envy and malice, or so it’s said. It’s very active at night.

Name: Banette
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: You may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A doll bore a grudge over being junked, and it became a Pokémon. It seeks the child that disowned it.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: If you have 13 or more Pokémon that have the Hide ‘n’ Sneak Ability in your discard pile, choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon and quadruple the number of damage counters on each of them.
Flavor-Text: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

Name: Litwick
Flavor-Text: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Lampent and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Lampent appears at the moment of death and promptly absorbs the spirit as it leaves the body.

Name: Mega Chandelure ex
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: If you have 4 or more Pokémon that have the Hide ‘n’ Sneak Ability in your discard pile, this attack does 140 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

Name: Marshadow
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: By slipping into the shadow of a martial arts master and copying their movements, this Pokémon learned the ultimate techniques.

Name: Annihilape
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Place 5 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Mankey
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Cranidos
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Cranidos toughen up their already sturdy heads by headbutting one another.

Name: Rampardos ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 150 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: By spinning its body, it can dig straight through the ground at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Mega Darkrai ex
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 110 more damage.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, it is Knocked Out.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Vullaby
Flavor-Text: Its healthy appetite leads to visible growth spurts. It often has to replace the bones it wears as its size increases.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Special Energy attached. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Watching from the sky, they attack weakened prey on the ground. They have a habit of decorating themselves with bones.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It drains foes’ will to fight by flashing light at them. It then takes the opportunity to hide itself away.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t Confused, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

Name: Nickit
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Morpeko ex
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zarude
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your Benched Pokémon have any Shadowy [D] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

Name: Maschiff
Flavor-Text: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

Name: Mabosstiff
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Chi-Yu
Effects: If this Pokémon has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Discard up to 2 Basic [M] Energy cards from your hand, and this attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Because it nests in bramble bushes, the feathers of its chicks grow hard from being scratched by thorns.

Name: Shieldon
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. It’s thought that Shieldon had few enemies thanks to its sturdy face.

Name: Bastiodon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to each of your Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have 2 or less Energy attached.
Flavor-Text: Bastiodon live in herds. When assaulted by enemies, they line up side by side and use their hard faces to block attacks.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [M] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They are found in ancient tombs. The patterns on their backs are said to be imbued with mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from Evolution Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Mega Excadrill ex
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 130 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pikipek
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Pikipek has strong muscles in its neck, so it won’t hurt itself even if it violently shakes its head.

Name: Trumbeak
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it encounters foes, it launches the many seeds stored in its beak in a radial burst.

Name: Toucannon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: Pairs of Toucannon are considered symbols of companionship, as these Pokémon will raise the temperature of their beaks to warm each other.

Name: Type: Null
Flavor-Text: It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.

Name: Silvally
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have no cards in your hand, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The final factor needed to release this Pokémon’s true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, search your deck for a Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Name: Antique Armor Fossil
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, all of your Pokémon take 10 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Antique Skull Fossil
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Backtrack Badge
Description: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of the [C] Pokémon this card is attached to, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Dark Bell
Description: Both Active non-[D] Pokémon are now Confused.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Fossil Quarry
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for up to 2 Item cards that have “Antique” in their name and put them onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Gladion’s Final Battle
Description:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Gwynn
Description: Discard up to 2 Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box from your hand, and draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Jett
Description: Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Misty’s Vitality
Description: Search your deck for up to 4 Basic [W] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rust Syndicate Grunt
Description: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tremendous Bomb
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to isn’t a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, is in the Active Spot, and takes 240 or more damage from an attack from your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon ex (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Shadowy [D] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [D] Energy.As long as the [D] Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to it by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.

Name: Voltaic [L] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [L] Energy.Attacks used by the [L] Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Fomantis
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Many Trainers give their Fomantis their own flowerpots so they can sunbathe in peace and quiet.

Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.

Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Brave Bangle
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn’t have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Dark Bell
Description: Both Active non-[D] Pokémon are now Confused.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Gladion’s Final Battle
Description:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Description: Discard up to 2 Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box from your hand, and draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Iron Defender
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, all of your [M] Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Misty’s Vitality
Description: Search your deck for up to 4 Basic [W] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rust Syndicate Grunt
Description: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tremendous Bomb
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to isn’t a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, is in the Active Spot, and takes 240 or more damage from an attack from your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon ex (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Mega Zeraora ex
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Chandelure ex
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Darkrai ex
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 110 more damage.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, it is Knocked Out.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Morpeko ex
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gladion’s Final Battle
Description:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Gwynn
Description: Discard up to 2 Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box from your hand, and draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 110 more damage.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, it is Knocked Out.
